---
title: "3.9 this Keyword"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### `this` means the current object

The keyword <span class="term">this</span> (当前对象) refers to the object whose method or constructor is currently running.

It is commonly used to:

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish instance variables from parameters with the same name
- pass the current object as an argument
- make code clearer when an instance variable is being used
:::

## `this.instanceVariable`

### Resolve same-name variables

```java
public class Person
{
    private String name;

    public Person(String name)
    {
        this.name = name;
    }
}
```

`this.name` means the instance variable. The plain `name` on the right side means the constructor parameter.

## Code Task

### Constructor with `this`

```java
public class BankAccount
{
    private String owner;
    private double balance;

    public BankAccount(String owner, double balance)
    {
        this.owner = owner;
        this.balance = balance;
    }
}
```

Trace: `this.owner` is the field of the newly created object.

## `this` as an Argument

### Pass the current object to another method

![this trace](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-9-this-keyword/thisTrace.png){fig-align="center" width="48%"}

Inside an instance method, `this` can be passed to a method that expects an object of the same class.

```java
public void compareWith(BankAccount other)
{
    helper.compare(this, other);
}
```

## Quick Check

### Trace same-name assignment

What happens if the constructor omits `this`?

```java
public Person(String name)
{
    name = name;
}
```

Answer: the parameter is assigned to itself. The instance variable is not updated.

Correct form:

```java
this.name = name;
```

## Groupwork Coding Challenge {.fit-small .image-fit}

### Bank Account

![Bank account challenge prompt](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-9-this-keyword/dollarSign.png){fig-align="center" width="22%"}

Starter:

```java
public class BankAccount
{
    private String owner;
    private double balance;

    public BankAccount(String owner, double balance)
    {
        this.owner = owner;
        this.balance = balance;
    }
}
```

Add `deposit`, `withdraw`, and `toString` methods.

## Method Example

### Use `this` only where it helps clarity

```java
public void deposit(double amount)
{
    if (amount > 0)
    {
        this.balance += amount;
    }
}
```

`this.balance` and `balance` refer to the same instance variable here, but `this` makes the object state explicit.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that `this` refers to the current object
- use `this.variableName` to distinguish a field from a parameter
- trace why `name = name` does not update the instance variable
- explain when `this` can be passed as an object argument
- use `this` for clarity without treating it as required everywhere
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
